title: Tingling Korean Chicken Tagine
description: Mustardy, shattering Korean chicken tagine served in a tagine pot with crisp plum sauce and en‑croute coating, finished like a patbingsu dessert for meal prep.
instructions: Heat sesame oil in a tagine pot over medium heat until shimmering.
Toss the chicken pieces in a bowl and season with salt, pepper, and whole grain mustard until coated.
In a separate bowl, mix plum sauce, apple cider vinegar, and gluten‑free flour until smooth.
Dip each chicken piece in the sauce mixture, then roll in crushed pistachios to create an en‑croute crust.
Add the coated chicken to the tagine pot and sprinkle with shredded sweet potato, ensuring a layer of the potato on top.
Cover the pot with its lid and reduce heat to low, allowing the sauce to thicken and the flavors to meld for 25 minutes.
During the last 5 minutes, stir in chopped cilantro and toast sesame seeds over the heated mixture.
Remove the pot from heat and let it rest for 5 minutes so the crust remains crisp.
Shave a generous amount of ice into a bowl and spoon the chilled chicken and sauce over the ice.
Garnish with additional pistachios and a drizzle of plum sauce before serving.
